iss_build.title: Set up a dedicated ISS combat build for Ch11 bosses — at least 2 Level II spells from matching schools

subtitle.text: Evocation for damage. Abjuration for shields. Ender for exits. Pick two. Master them. The bosses don't pause.

description: CH.11 · THE COMBAT BUILD
▸ By Ch11 you have the spell schools scroll.
You have the mana capacity.
You have the arcane codex.
This quest is about building one coherent
combat loadout before entering the Cataclysm chain.
◈ BOSS-SPECIFIC ISS SCHOOL PAIRINGS
└ ✦ vs. Ignited Revenant (fire)
Holy: Sacred Ground (healing vs. fire DoT)
Evocation: Fireball (fire type = zero resist on Revenant)
Wait — Revenant IS fire. Use: Ender (Blink away)
+ Holy (Smite, undead bonus if applicable)
└ ✦ vs. Ignis (fire boss)
Evocation: Void Tentacles (non-fire damage)
Conjuration: Summon Ally (distraction)
└ ✦ vs. Annihilator (void boss)
Eldritch: bonus effectiveness vs void entities
Abjuration: Magic Shield (absorb void zone hit)
└ ✦ vs. Monstrosity (Nether physical boss)
Evocation: all spells apply
Holy: Divine Smite for burst window
◈ MANA MANAGEMENT
└ Ring of Mana: +100 Max Mana (wear 2 for +200)
└ Ancient Codex: +200 Max Mana + 20% CDR
Best base item for sustained combat casting
└ Research time bonus: each scroll studied reduces
school-specific casting cost (~5% per school)
└ Mana regen: Ring of Recovery + Puffish Skills mage track
◈ LEVEL SCALING
└ Level III spells: highest damage, but high mana cost
Use Level II for sustained fights (more casts per pool)
Use Level III for burst windows (boss phase transitions)
└ Priority Level III spells for Ch11:
Evocation Fireball III, Holy Divine Smite III,
Ender Blink III (fastest escape in any situation)
▸ The Cataclysm bosses were not designed for melee.
The Engineers who made the portal system
were spellcasters.
They left their builds in the scroll records.
The Wizard drops confirm this.
▶ Set up a dedicated ISS combat build [check]
▶ Obtain a Ring of Mana
◇ Two rings of mana = +200 max mana base.
Add Ancient Codex (+200 more).
That is 400 bonus mana on top of your level-based pool.
Most Level II spells cost 80-120 mana.
Do the math before your first Cataclysm fight.
